race.name: Maraton Franja BTC City
race.tagline: Europe's oldest gran fondo. 43 editions through the Slovenian heartland since 1982, named after a secret WWII partisan hospital hidden in a mountain gorge.

vitals.registration: Open registration via franja.org. Early bird EUR 44 (until March 31), regular EUR 55 (April 1 - May 15). No lottery. UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier.
vitals.feed_zones: Multiple refreshment stations along the route with food, drinks, and energy supplies. Refreshment point at the top of the Kladje climb.
vitals.cutoff_time: Road closures enforced — Godovic-Idrija closed 9:40-13:05, Cerkno-Kladje-Trebija closed 10:40-15:00
route_options: Gran Fondo: 156 km (97 mi), 2,822 m (9,258 ft) elevation gain
Medio Fondo: 97 km (60 mi), 1,625 m (5,331 ft) elevation gain

climate.description: Mid-June in Ljubljana and the surrounding countryside brings warm, generally pleasant cycling conditions. Average temperatures of 20C with highs reaching 25-28C and lows around 14C. The Ljubljana Basin sits at 300m elevation with the course climbing to a maximum of 801m, so temperature variation along the route is modest. June is one of the wetter months in Slovenia with approximately 125mm of precipitation spread across 13 days, meaning afternoon thunderstorms are a real possibility.
challenges: Afternoon thunderstorms can develop — June is Ljubljana's wettest month
Warm conditions (25-28C) on exposed road sections demand steady hydration
Descents after the Kladje climb can feel cool if wet weather moves in
Long daylight hours (15+ hours) are an advantage, not a challenge

terrain.primary: Paved Slovenian provincial and back-country roads
terrain.surface: All paved throughout. Slovenian provincial roads range from well-maintained primary routes to narrower back-country lanes. The descent from Kladje includes sections with rough road paving. One short macadam (packed gravel) section near Povodje approaching the finish.
features: Ljubljana Basin: flat departure through the capital before climbing begins
Vrhnika Hill: 4.6 km at 4.1% average, 7% maximum — the opener
Logatec Plateau: rolling terrain through the Karst region
Godovic descent to Idrija: dangerous, technical descent — caution required
Kladje Climb: 7 km at 6.3% average, 10% maximum — the crux of the race
Kladje descent: 10 km, rough road surface, dangerous — the most technical section
Skofja Loka to Vodice: rolling terrain through medieval town
Final approach: flat run through Gameljne back to BTC City Ljubljana

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 70. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 70.

course_description.character: A 156 km loop through the Ljubljana Basin and Julian Alps foothills. The route leaves Ljubljana, climbs gently over the Vrhnika Hill, traverses the Logatec Plateau, descends through the historic mercury-mining town of Idrija, then tackles the defining Kladje climb — 7 km at 6.3% average with a 10% maximum pitch. After a long, rough descent through alpine forest, the route passes through medieval Skofja Loka before rolling back to Ljubljana across pastoral terrain. The course is diverse: urban start, rolling plateau, alpine forest climbs, historic towns, and flat agricultural finish. Maximum elevation is 801m — this is a foothills event, not an Alpine epic.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Kladje climb: 7 km at 6.3% average with a maximum gradient of 10%. It arrives at approximately km 90 after the plateau crossings and the Idrija valley, on legs that have already absorbed 60+ km of rolling terrain. The climb itself is honest — no false flats, no respite sections. But the real test comes immediately after: a 10 km descent on rough road surface that demands concentration when you most want to coast.
suffering_zones: Logatec Plateau to Idrija — Rolling and Descending
Kladje Descent to Skofja Loka — The Dangerous Descent
Vodice to Ljubljana — The Run Home

logistics.airport: Ljubljana Joze Pucnik Airport (LJU) — 26 km from city center, 30-40 min by bus (EUR 4) or taxi. Limited international routes but growing. Alternative airports: Venice Marco Polo (VCE) — 4 hours. Vienna (VIE) — 3.5 hours. Munich (MUC) — 5 hours. Zagreb (ZAG) — 2 hours.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Stay in Ljubljana city center — compact, walkable, and well-connected to the BTC City start/finish by public transport or a short ride. Hotels EUR 60-120/night, Airbnb options plentiful. The city center is charming with excellent restaurants and cafes. No need for a car — Ljubljana is one of Europe's most cycling-friendly capitals. For a quieter option, stay in Skofja Loka (on the course, 30 min from Ljubljana) for a medieval town atmosphere.
logistics.food: Slovenian cuisine is a crossroads of Italian, Austrian, and Balkan influences. Struklji (rolled dumplings), potica (nut roll), jota (bean stew), local wines from Goriska Brda. Ljubljana's restaurant scene is excellent and affordable. Carb loading is effortless. Feed zones on the course provide food, drinks, and energy supplies at multiple points including the Kladje summit.

history.origin_story: The first Maraton Franja took place on July 22, 1982, with approximately 700 participants — most on regular, non-competitive bicycles. The event was named after the Franja Partisan Hospital, a secret WWII medical facility hidden in the Pasice gorge near Cerkno. Built in December 1943, the hospital was managed by Dr. Franja Bojc Bidovec and treated wounded partisans and Allied forces in complete secrecy, protected by retractable bridges, minefields, and camouflaged buildings. The marathon route passes near this UNESCO-nominated site, paying tribute to the courage of its medical staff. From those 700 inaugural riders, the event grew steadily through Slovenian independence (1991), reached record participation of 1,886 in 2002, and was selected as a founding member of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series in 2011. Over 150,000 cyclists from 80+ countries have completed the event.
notable_moments: 1982: Inaugural edition — July 22, approximately 700 participants on mostly non-competitive bicycles
1991: Continued through Slovenian independence — the event predates the nation itself
2002: Record participation of 1,886 in the main marathon, plus first Family Marathon
2011: Selected as founding member of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series
Lifetime: 150,000+ finishers from 80+ countries, including 23,000+ women
history.reputation: The quiet elder of European gran fondos. Not the flashiest, not the hardest, but one of the most enduring — running since before Slovenia was even an independent country. A founding pillar of the UCI Gran Fondo World Series and a Slovenian national cycling institution.

biased_opinion.summary: Maraton Franja is not the hardest gran fondo, not the most scenic, not the most famous. What it is, without qualification, is one of the most genuine. Running since 1982 — before 'gran fondo' was even a recognized category — this Slovenian institution has quietly built a 43-year legacy of accessible, well-organized road cycling through the Ljubljana Basin and Julian Alps foothills. Named after a secret WWII partisan hospital hidden in a mountain gorge, the event carries historical weight that no Italian monument or French classic can replicate. The Kladje climb will test you. The descent will scare you. The finish in Ljubljana will satisfy you. And the EUR 44 entry fee will make you wonder why everyone else charges three times as much.
strengths: 43+ continuous editions since 1982 — one of Europe's oldest gran fondos
UCI Gran Fondo World Series founding member with World Championship qualifying
Exceptional value: EUR 44-55 for a fully supported, UCI-sanctioned event
Ljubljana is affordable, accessible, and genuinely charming as a host city
weaknesses: Maximum elevation of 801m means no Alpine drama — this is a foothills event
The 10 km descent from Kladje has rough road surface requiring caution
International name recognition lags behind Italian and French gran fondos
June rain risk: Ljubljana's wettest month brings afternoon thunderstorm potential
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want a UCI Gran Fondo World Series qualifier that does not require a second mortgage or a lottery win, Maraton Franja is the answer. Four decades of Slovenian organizational pride, a meaningful historical connection, and a EUR 44 entry fee that includes lunch. The climbs are honest, the countryside is beautiful, and the Slovenian cycling community will make you feel welcome. This is gran fondo cycling as it was meant to be — unpretentious, affordable, and built to last.

final_verdict.one_liner: Europe's oldest gran fondo. 43 editions since 1982. UCI World Series founding member. EUR 44 entry. The Slovenian institution that proves longevity beats hype.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you want a well-organized UCI gran fondo qualifier at a fraction of the cost of Italian or French alternatives. Yes if the historical connection to a WWII partisan hospital resonates. Yes if you want to discover Slovenia — an underrated European cycling destination. Skip if you need Alpine drama — the maximum elevation is 801m. Skip if you need a globally recognized name on your cycling resume.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a comparable UCI qualifier with more climbing: La Marmotte (France). If you want European heritage without the Alps: Nove Colli (Italy). If you want another Central European UCI gran fondo: Tour de Pologne Amatorow (Poland).
